assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes

New file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` is purely additive (3 new test functions). Combined classification: MIXED. |'

assertion_results.evidence: report.md Structural Assessment table: '| Test functions | +1 (`test_recommend_purls_dedup` added) | -1 (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` removed) |'. Also Semantic Assessment section 1: 'Removed `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`: This test verified that qualifier variants appeared as separate entries in the response.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Structural Assessment: '| Assertion specificity | -- | -1 (assertion in `test_recommend_purls_basic` changed from fully qualified PURL `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"` to versioned PURL `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"` -- less specific expected value) |'. Semantic Assessment section 2: 'Assertion relaxation in `test_recommend_purls_basic`: The assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL string (with `?repository_url=...&type=jar`) to checking a versioned-only PURL string (without qualifiers)... This is a reductive signal because the old assertion would have detected regressions in qualifier formatting, while the new assertion cannot.'

assertion_results.evidence: report.md Structural Assessment: '**New file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`** Purely additive: +3 test functions, +12 assertions. New files are inherently additive and do not require sub-agent analysis.'

The report only presents findings.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Structural Assessment explicitly states 'Comparing base-branch version against PR-branch version:' and enumerates concrete file-level signals: function names added/removed (test_recommend_purls_dedup, test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), specific assertion values (PURL strings with and without qualifiers), assertion counts (+4, -1). The Semantic Assessment references test function content and coverage targets ('This test verified that qualifier variants appeared as separate entries', 'the old assertion would have detected regressions in qualifier formatting').
